articles.name: Bibles & Resources
articles.title: Bibles & Resources
articles.body:
Bibles

21 Days With God – Primarily used at Fields of Faith as a next step for athletes who were challenged to read God’s Word after the event. It includes one chapter per day through the Gospel of John. It also includes The FOUR. https://fcagear.com/fca-21-days-w-god/

The Sports Bible – This full-text Bible includes FCA content such as The FOUR, The CORE, Get to Know FCA, Get Involved with FCA and the Faith Response Tool, as well as QR codes that digitally link the reader to more FCA resources. This Bible is used for both coaches, athletes and competitors of all ages. This is a low price-point Bible that serves the needs for outreach events like Fields of Faith. https://fcagear.com/fca-sports-bible/

The Sports New Testament – This multi-sport New Testament features the FOUR, E3 Discipleship Training, and 12 character traits for the Christian competitor as well as QR codes to link the reader to video testimonies, devotionals and more. https://fcagear.com/2023-fca-sports-new-testament-bible-csb/

Resources

THE CORE - In THE CORE, you will learn about 8 essential practices for strengthening your faith in Christ including how to join God’s team, live with his power, train spiritually, communicate with God, study his playbook, team up with others, know your role, and share with others. https://fcagear.com/the-core/

Fields of Faith Handbook

articles.name: Closeout Guide
articles.title: Closeout Guide
articles.body:
Administrative Paperwork

  1. Submit any Abuse Reporting Forms
    1. Follow FCA’s abuse reporting protocol as outlined in the Risk Management Manual.
  2. Submit any Injury/Illness/Incident Reports
    1. Follow FCA’s abuse reporting protocol as outlined in the Risk Management Manual.
  3. Submit the FOF Closeout Report Form: Login to “Manage Your Field” > fieldsoffaith.com
  4. Submit Vendor Payments and/or Honorariums
  5. Share all Faith Decisions with local FCA staff, FCA Huddles and appropriate local church contacts
    1. Create an intentional follow-up plan to organize Faith Decisions and other pertinent information

Evaluation

Typically, after a Fields of Faith event, many ask: How can we grow our Fields of Faith event next year? It’s an important question to ask as you reflect upon God’s calling in your life as you consider how Fields of Faith can create a movement to change the culture in your community.

When thinking about FCA’s E3 strategy of Engage Equip Empower, an even deeper question is: How can we multiply Fields of Faith next year? This is a fantastic question to discern and process alongside your trusted volunteer team and fellow FCA staff. Discipleship is all about personal transformation and public multiplication. How can Fields of Faith be a catalyst for discipleship until the next Fields of Faith? Is there another location (or locations) that can launch next year? Who is God empowering to lead and in what capacities?

However the Lord leads you, always reflect upon how your Fields of Faith is staying true to discipleship at and after the program. Continue to trust that as you seek God’s heart for your Field, He will continue to provide the harvest.

articles.name: Marketing and Gear
articles.title: Marketing and Gear
articles.body:
Develop and Launch A Marketing Plan

  • Prioritize getting the word out about Fields of Faith and get ready to invite like crazy! Imagine filling the space with as many people as can fit in the venue to be encouraged by the hope of the gospel and challenged to read God’s Word!

  • How will people know about Fields of Faith? Where will they be invited? As a Fields of Faith Leadership Team, meet early in the planning process and discern how you will market your Fields of Faith event.

  • Consider launching your promotional plan about 3-5 weeks before the date of your FOF, and continue to promote more often as the event gets closer.

  • FCA Staff who have an FCA Canva Pro account will have access to templates and designs created specifically for promoting Fields of Faith. You may use these resources for printed and digital promotion.

  • Canva FAQ’s: https://teamnet.fca.org/esc?id=kb_article&table=kb_knowledge&sysparm_article=KB0010212

  • Canva Training Video: https://teamnet.fca.org/esc?id=kb_article&table=kb_knowledge&sysparm_article=KB0010214

Know Your Community and Invite!

  • Effective promotion starts with your team and it starts with knowing your community and audience.

  • Consider which strategies will be most effective in getting the word out.

  • Social media is a great way to meet your audience where they already are an effective tool and usually an easy place to connect with many people.

  • Consider utilizing social media platforms where FCA is already a familiar presence. Does FCA have a platform on Instagram, Facebook, X, Snapchat etc.?

  • Posters and invitation cards can also be useful to extending an invitation to Fields of Faith.

  • Consider hanging posters in places like classrooms, locker rooms, churches or community spaces where coaches, athletes and students will likely see them. Be sure to seek any necessary approval before hanging posters.

  • Invitation cards or rack cards offer a personal invite to FoF and an are easy way to quickly provide details about the event in the hands of anyone who is interested.

  • You might also leverage connections with the community by encourage local FCA Huddles, clubs, leagues, church partners and youth groups to join your team as you hang posters, re-post on social, and spread the word about the event!

  • Don’t miss a chance to spread the word! What other unique marketing opportunities can you take advantage of to reach your community?

  • Fields of Faith brand guide: https://www.fieldsoffaith.com/resources-before/

Develop and Launch A Marketing Plan

  • Another way to promote Fields of Faith is through wearing gear!

  • FCA Gear offers a great selection of FoF t-shirts, as well the option to customize t-shirts for your event!

  • FCA Gear: https://fcagear.com/collections/fields-of-faith-/

  • If you order t-shirts for your event, encourage your FoF team to lead strong and wear the Fields of Faith t-shirt the day of the event to spread the word to friends and family on their teams, at school and in the community.

  • Order FoF gear for your entire team to wear during the event. Consider selecting different colors for the Ministry Response Team to be easily identified during the program.

  • Gear is a great way to extend the experience of Fields of Faith and remind your team, and attendees of the impact and the decision they made that night! Purchase extra T-shirts or The FOUR bracelets to use as giveaways!

  • Be sure to place an order with FCA Gear at least 30 days prior to the date you need your gear in hand to ensure you have it ready for the event.

articles.name: Prepare Chruch Partnership
articles.title: Prepare Chruch Partnership
articles.body:
Many opportunities exist for FCA to partner with local churches for Fields of Faith. Here are several examples.

Pray/Inform

  • Invite local churches to join your team in prayer leading up to, and during, Fields of Faith

Give

  • Ask churches to financially partner to underwrite the cost of Fields of Faith through sponsorships (if applicable)
  • Ask churches for in-kind donations for equipment they own such as audio/visual equipment, staging, tables, games, etc.

Promote

  • Provide necessary information for churches to invite their congregation to do the following:
  • Invite their student ministries to attend
  • Invite their congregation to attend and/or invite other athletes
  • Invite their congregation to serve

Serve

  • Ask churches to help fill several volunteer roles on your team:
  • Invite pastors to serve as Closing Speakers
  • Invite the worship team to serve on the Worship Team
  • Invite trusted congregants to serve on the Ministry Response Team
  • Invite other trusted congregants to serve in secondary roles (Set-Up Team, Welcome Team, etc.)

Follow-Up

  • Create a list of churches that support Fields of Faith as a recommended list of churches for those attending your event that want to get connected to a local church

articles.name: Role Descriptions
articles.title: Role Descriptions
articles.body:
FCA Staff Roles

FCA staff may serve as a Field Leader, Ministry Response Coordinator, or on the Ministry Response team. Primarily, these positions should be fulfilled by Mobilized Volunteers though they can be filled by FCA staff if needed.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and serve Fields of Faith Team, prior, during and post event
  • Secure event venue
  • Certify Fields of Faith
  • Obtain Certificate of Insurance (COI)
  • Ensure all primary leadership positions are led by Mobilized Volunteers
  • Train the Speakers and Ministry Response Team
  • Develop the Ministry Response Plan and Follow-up plan
  • Submit/send any Abuse Report Forms or Injury/Illness/Incident Report Forms to the Support Center

Primary Roles

Fields of Faith requires several key roles. At FCA, we believe that these roles should be filled by individuals who are Mobilized Volunteers. A mobilized volunteer is someone who has completed FCA’s E3 Overview Course, has an approved Ministry Leader Application (MLA), and leads in an FCA certified ministry program.

  • Field Leader: An adult leader who has strong organizational and leadership skills, has completed the Fields of Faith Training in FCAU and is committed to leading through the filter of FCA (Discipleship Filter p. 40 E3 playbook). The Field Leader will work closely with FCA Staff to plan and execute Fields of Faith while leading and training the Fields of Faith Leadership Team.
  • Speakers: A leader who has shown knowledge of scripture with an emphasis on the Gospel. Committed to speaking through the filter of FCA (Discipleship Filter p. 40 E3 playbook). You should consider athletes, coaches, pastors, and/or those with the gift of evangelism. The goal would be to use peer influencers who can clearly share their testimony making much of Jesus and less of themselves.
  • Athlete Speaker: An athlete and peer leader who can clearly and confidently share their testimony. They are a disciple who make disciples. As an ambassador of Christ, they Engage with God daily and strive to meet others where they are on their spiritual journey. They are Equipped with God’s Word and are growing in Christ, and they are Empowered to help others experience salvation and grow in their faith and share Him with others.
  • Closing Speaker: An athlete, coach, pastor and/or someone with the gift of evangelism. This leader will close the event by wrapping up what the Athlete Speaker(s) shared, clearly communicating the Gospel message by using The FOUR, and making an invitation for a faith response. They will lead those who are ready to place their trust in Jesus in a prayer to believe and confess that Jesus is Lord and surrender their life to Him. The Closing Speaker will conclude the event by introducing the challenge to commit to reading a chapter of John each day for 21 days. The Closing Speaker direct anyone who made a Faith Response or needs prayer to the Ministry Response Team.
  • Ministry Response Team: A group of individuals who have Gospel clarity and are E3 trained to lead and pray with athletes and attendees by biblically responding to any questions they have or faith decisions they make at a Fields of Faith event. They are skilled at capturing faith decisions (via the Faith Response Tool) and knowledgeable about FCA resources and next steps.
  • Ministry Response Coordinator: The Leader of the Ministry Response Team ensures that all those on the Ministry Response Team have gospel clarity and are E3 trained. The Leader prepares the Ministry Response Team with best practices on how to lead and pray with athletes and respond to their questions. The Leader instructs the team on how to capture faith decisions and trains on FCA resources, programs and next steps.
  • Worship Leader (Team): An individual or group of individuals who have gospel clarity and are E3 trained and are gifted in leading others in worship. They can confidently and clearly lead and engage with others in worship by selecting appropriate songs for the event.

Mobilized Volunteer Links

  • E3 Overview Course (https://university.fca.org/register/)
  • Ministry Leader Application (see https://mla.fca.org/)

Secondary Roles

Fields of Faith may also necessitate additional secondary roles. Depending on the size of your Fields of Faith event, you may want to consider any of the following roles. It is important for these individuals to complete an MLA/VA prior to serving.

  • Emcee
  • Set-Up Team
  • Welcome / Greeting Team
  • Security Team
  • Production Team (Sound/Audio/Visual)
  • Food / Games Team
  • Gear / Resources Team

Volunteers: Must have MLA/VA on file. Participates at any level of the FCA ministry by giving their time, talent, and treasure. Volunteers may serve in a variety of ways including, but not limited to welcoming /closing at entrance of event, handing out event resources, serving food, etc.

articles.name: Sample Program Schedule
articles.title: Sample Program Schedule
articles.body:
BEFORE PROGRAM

2:00–Stage setup, A/V setup, sound check with band

5:30–Volunteer Check-In

5:45–Volunteer Meeting/Prayer Time

6:15–Gates open: Welcome Team

6:30–Start Music: (Playlist)

PROGRAM

6:50–Live Music Kickoff: Worship Team (12 minutes – high energy)

7:02–Welcome: Emcee (Coach, Volunteer, or Staff) (5 minutes)

  • Engage crowd, announcements, shoutouts, etc.
  • Introduce Worship Team & prayer: Emcee (1 minute)

7:08–Opening Prayer: Athlete Speaker or Coach (2 minutes)

7:10–Worship Music: Worship Team

7:16–Intro Speaker: Emcee

7:17–Josiah’s Challenge (FOF Purpose): Athlete Speaker or Coach

7:20–Intro Speaker/Prayer: Emcee

7:21–Testimony: Athlete Speaker

7:26–Worship Music: Worship Team

7:31–Intro Speaker/Prayer: Emcee

7:32–Testimony: Athlete Speaker

7:37–Worship Music: Worship Team

7:42–Intro Speaker/Prayer: Emcee

7:43–Gospel Message: Closing Speaker

8:08–Invitation: Closing Speaker + Worship Team

  • Response Time: Ministry Response Team

8:18–Worship Music: Worship Team

  • Continued Response Time as Needed: Ministry Response Team

8:30–Closing Prayer/Dismissal Instructions: Emcee

AFTER PROGRAM

9:00–Tear Down

articles.name: How to Submit your Post Fields of Faith Report
articles.title: How to Submit your Post Fields of Faith Report
articles.body:
Once your Fields of Faith is over, it’s important to celebrate and share what happened at your event by completing the Fields of Faith Report Form.

You will find a link to this form in the confirmation email you received after listing your field on fieldsoffaith.org.

 Attention
Please complete the report form as soon as possible following your Fields of Faith.

The report form will ask you to share the number of people who attended your Fields of Faith, as well as how many Faith Decisions were made.

We can’t wait to hear about what God did at your Fields of Faith event!

articles.name: What is Fields of Faith
articles.title: What is Fields of Faith
articles.body:
Definition:
Fields of Faith is a certified ministry program where athletes gather at sport environments in their communities to challenge their peers to grow in their relationship with Jesus Christ and His Church.

As a certified ministry program, Fields of Faith executes the E3 Strategy by providing athletes the opportunity to engage with the Good News, equip in God’s Word, and empower with the Great Commission. Fields of Faith is an onramp to discipleship.

Fields of Faith is a movement to change the culture in a community.

History

In 2002, Oklahoma FCA Area Director Jeff Martin prayed about what to do with his heartfelt frustration regarding the temptations and spiritual battles facing the “spectator generation” youth. God led him to 2 Chronicles 34 for the answer. King Josiah, an influential teenager, gathered his people and challenged them to read God’s Word. As a result, they changed their culture.

“Then the king stood at his post and made a covenant in the LORD’s presence to follow the LORD and to keep his commands, his decrees, and his statutes with all his heart and with all his soul in order to carry out the words of the covenant written in this book.”—2 Chronicles 34:31

King Josiah became king early in his life. By the time he was 16 years old, he began to seek God. When he was 20, he went into action. He began to purge his nation of detestable idols and images. When he turned 26, he began to clean out and repair the temple of God. The Book of the Law (scriptures) was discovered and read to King Josiah. His heart was convicted of sin, and he changed his ways. The young king challenged his nation to do the same, and it wholeheartedly followed God all the days of King Josiah’s reign.

In 2004, the Josiah-influenced dream came true when more than 6,000 students gathered on school athletic fields throughout Oklahoma, Texas and Kansas for Fields of Faith. Through the student-led events, more than 100 students made decisions for Christ. Since that day, hundreds of thousands of people have attended Fields of Faith and thousands have received Jesus Christ as their Lord and Savior.

Today

As FCA pursues its mission to lead every coach and athlete into a growing relationship with Jesus Christ and His church, the movement of Fields of Faith has expanded to reach anyone, anywhere, anytime.

  • Anyone: Fields of Faith is led by athletes and is for any athlete no matter where, how, or at what level they compete.
  • Anywhere: Fields of Faith serves athletes in any sport environment and any country.
  • Any time: Fields of Faith is not locked to a single date and can occur any time year-round.

Together, we celebrate the many who have been forever transformed through the ministry program of Fields of Faith, and we look ahead with great anticipation to the future for how Fields of Faith can serve athletes across the globe at any level, anywhere and at any time.

“[The gospel] is bearing fruit and growing all over the world.”—Colossians 1:6

Why "Fields"?

“Open your eyes and look at the fields, because they are ready for harvest.”—John 4:35

In the language of the Bible, the concept of a field holds significant spiritual implications. A field was connected to agriculture—the place where people cultivated soil, planted seeds, watered, worked, and trusted God for growth and harvest.

1 Corinthians 3:6-9—Sow…reap…God gave the growth. “For we are God’s coworkers. You are God’s field, God’s building.”

Matthew 13:24—KOG compared to a man who sowed good seed in his field…31 mustard seed in a field

Galatians 6:8-9—sowing and reaping, if don’t give up

Regardless of what environment a Fields of Faith is held—whether it’s at a gymnasium, pool, racetrack or stadium—think about Jesus’ words when he challenges his disciples: “Open your eyes and look at the fields, because they are ready for harvest.” (John 4:35)

Where is the spiritual field God is calling you to? In other words, where is the place of influence that God is moving? Who are the athletes and coaches in your life that God is calling you to reach?

Are your eyes open to it?

Fields of Faith can be your moment to be part of the movement to change the culture in your community.

Fields of Faith is more than just a program. It’s a catalytic experience to create a disciple-making movement where you live.

You, too, can take a King Josiah stand by challenging athletes to grow in their relationship with Jesus Christ and His church.

Look at the fields. Look at your field. It’s ready for harvest.
